page: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with the instance scoring in the top 10% on Passmark CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier status for both cached small-block and uncached large-block reads. The hardware-accelerated instruction sets contribute to top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX capabilities deliver top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large LLM models, though text generation speeds remain in the average to strong range.

Given its compute-heavy profile and lack of local storage, the c8i.24xlarge offers high resource density for CPU-bound tasks but requires external storage considerations that may impact overall architecture cost. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory but lacks local storage. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, driving top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, database operations, and static web serving. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, ranking in the top 10% for both cached and uncached operations. While single-core stress tests perform in the average range, multi-core execution and software compilation are highly efficient. This instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, including software compilation, database management, and CPU-based large language model prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring advanced matrix extensions and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier multi-core performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, database workloads, and CPU-based LLM inference
